chunk_summary: This section outlines a staged approach to adolescent obesity treatment, emphasizing BMI-for-age staging, comorbidity risk assessment, and escalation to specialist care, including pharmacotherapy and bariatric surgery for severe cases.
learning_objectives: Understand the staging process for adolescent obesity treatment using BMI-for-age and comorbidity risk.
Identify measurable behavioral targets for initial outpatient management of adolescent obesity.
Learn criteria for escalation to pharmacotherapy and bariatric surgery in adolescents with severe obesity.
Recognize the importance of specialist monitoring in advanced obesity care.
key_facts: Class 2 obesity: BMI ≥120% to <140% of the 95th percentile or BMI ≥35 to <40 kg/m².
Class 3 obesity: BMI ≥140% of the 95th percentile or BMI ≥40 kg/m².
Pharmacotherapy is recommended for adolescents ≥12 years with class 2 obesity plus major comorbidities or class 3 obesity.
Bariatric surgery is considered for adolescents >12 years with class 2 obesity plus significant comorbidities or class 3 obesity after 6 months of failed intensive lifestyle and pharmacotherapy.
Initial outpatient targets include 60 minutes/day of moderate-to-vigorous activity, 8–10 hours of sleep, and zero sugar-sweetened beverages.
reference_quote: Pharmacotherapy may be considered in adolescents ≥12 years with class 2 obesity plus major comorbidities, or class 3 obesity, as an adjunct to comprehensive lifestyle therapy and only with specialist monitoring. (Source: IAP guidelines for childhood obesity)
document_key_topics: Specialist care escalation for severe obesity
